title: Hyundai workers strike as humanoid robot plans enter labour negotiations
friendlyTitle: Hyundai’s robot plans become part of a worker strike
summary: Unionised Hyundai workers in South Korea have begun partial strikes after negotiations covering pay, retirement and the company’s humanoid-robot plans failed to reach agreement. Hyundai intends to begin deploying Boston Dynamics Atlas robots in a U.S. factory in 2028 and has discussed a much larger eventual fleet across its plants.
friendlySummary: Hyundai workers in South Korea are holding partial strikes after talks about pay, retirement and future humanoid robots broke down. Hyundai plans to start using Atlas robots in a U.S. factory in 2028.
whyItMatters: Physical AI is moving from demonstrations into workforce planning, where the practical questions are hours, wages, retraining and how gains are shared. Businesses adopting robotics should involve workers early and measure safety and task performance rather than treating headcount reduction as the only outcome.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Robots can change jobs, working hours and pay. Companies should talk with workers early, provide training and prove that new systems are safe and useful.
